## EdgeLLM 0.1.0

First release 🎉 — run local LLMs on Android with a clean Kotlin API.

```kotlin
val session = llm.load(Catalog.qwen25_05b)
session.chat(listOf(ChatMessage.user("Why is the sky blue?")))
    .collect { event -> /* stream tokens */ }
```

### Highlights

- **Kotlin-first API** — `catalog → checkFit → download → load → chat`, all suspend/Flow based
- **Model delivery** — resumable downloads with SHA-256 verification and a disk-space guard; curated catalog (Qwen 2.5 0.5B/1.5B, Llama 3.2 1B) or any GGUF URL
- **RAM fit check** — know before loading whether a model fits the device
- **Streaming chat** — conversations formatted with the model's own chat template; cancel the collection to stop generation
- **Backend routing** — `Backend.AUTO` picks CPU or GPU (OpenCL) per device, verifies what actually loaded, and falls back to CPU on driver failure
- **llama.cpp inside** — statically linked (arm64-v8a), native exceptions contained, logs bridged to logcat
- **Sample app** — complete Compose chat app showing the recommended lifecycle

### Installation

```kotlin
implementation("io.github.rezzaghi:edgellm-core:0.1.0")
runtimeOnly("io.github.rezzaghi:edgellm-engine-llamacpp:0.1.0")
```

Artifacts are on GitHub Packages (auth token required — see [Getting started](https://github.com/rezzaghi/edgellm/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md)). Requires minSdk 28, arm64-v8a.

### Known limitations

- GitHub Packages requires a personal access token even for public packages; Maven Central is planned
- GPU inference is verified on Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 (Adreno/OpenCL); other SoCs conservatively run on CPU
- One generation at a time per session
